# Paulino welcomes 9k studes with new building; rates GC as highly university-ready
[Image: Paulino welcomes 9k studes with new building; rates GC as highly university-ready]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, October 18, 2025**
GORDON COLLEGE MAIN CAMPUS — Coinciding with PAGLALAYAG 2025 (more commonly known as Apertura 2025), the newly acquired Gordon College Annex Building, located at Lower Kalaklan, Olongapo, was formally inaugurated on August 11.
In an interview with The Forefront, Olongapo City Mayor Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. “wanted to be practical” in e

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready]
iew with The Forefront, Olongapo City Mayor Atty. Rolen C. Paulino Jr. “wanted to be practical” in expanding Gordon College (GC), citing several important considerations.
Paulino believes that students applied to Gordon College for quality education, a main reason to attract 9,500 students this Academic Year (A.Y).
As the A.Y. 2025-2026 begins, the local chief executive highlighted their desire to make ongoing improvements to the current building and structure, ensuring new equipment is added as needed.
“We want to make sure that everything goes accordingly. Gusto talaga naming mag-expand,” th

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready]
 needed.
“We want to make sure that everything goes accordingly. Gusto talaga naming mag-expand,” the mayor said.
The back-to-back mayor of Olongapo City also considered improving and adding programs that would need more equipment and facilities.
“Tinitingnan din natin ‘yong possibility na mag-expand pa tayo kasi may clamor din to add additional [programs], whether more medical courses or engineering, lahat ‘yan pinag-iisipan namin,” the mayor added.
Now, the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) has officially moved to the newly inaugurated annex building of the institution.
When asked ab

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready]
(CBA) has officially moved to the newly inaugurated annex building of the institution.
When asked about the institution’s readiness for university status, the Board of Trustees Chairperson gave the institution a rating of 8 to 9 out of 10, stressing that while “the qualifications [are] there and personnel [are] there”, there remains the challenge of “accumulating enough resources to qualify.”
Moreover, the chairperson spoke of the institution being already at that point as “the quality of education [that Gordon College] provides is at par with some of the universities.”
“Sa results ng [board]

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready]
ion [that Gordon College] provides is at par with some of the universities.”
“Sa results ng [board] exams natin, we’re always competing with the best universities,” he added.
Furthermore, the mayor underscored the need to instill professionalism and discipline among students through the institution, aiming to make them employable and ready to become future managers. He also nurtured and wished for the students to apply values of professionalism and intelligence to work hand-in-hand when enrolled within the institution.
“[Please] know that the city government is here to support the institution

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/paulino-welcomes-9k-studes-with-new-building-rates-gc-as-highly-university-ready]
 within the institution.
“[Please] know that the city government is here to support the institution and the students. The city is always proud of Gordon College. It’s the home of many top-notchers, many successful community leaders, both in business and society. Mataas ang accreditation natin, we are internationally ranked, [and] we are very proud of the school and very proud of its students,” he concluded. | via Keane Rafael Abdon/The FOREFRONT

# Gordon College Celebrates World Teachers’ Day
[Image: Gordon College Celebrates World Teachers’ Day]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, October 7, 2025**
GORDON COLLEGE MAIN BLDG—The Office of the Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) hosted a World Teachers’ Day Celebration, together with various student organizations, which aims to honor the dedicated instructors of the institution on October 6, 2025, at Gordon College Main Bldg—Function Hall.
The event began with an opening prayer and remarks from Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, Gordon College President, and Dr. Darwi

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day]
h an opening prayer and remarks from Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, Gordon College President, and Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs.
“We cannot have a lavish lifestyle in teaching. But your dedication and passion will definitely [inaudible] through saying our students’ success— changing their lives, especially those who are underprivileged students, and you are making them a story,” said Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, showing her gratitude for the effort of Gordon College’s faculty.
Dr. Darwin P. Paguio also expressed to the faculty that the celebration isn’t just about their perseve

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day]
. Darwin P. Paguio also expressed to the faculty that the celebration isn’t just about their perseverance, but also to take care of all the hard work in the classrooms as they invest in nurturing curiosity that will nourish them for a lifetime.
After the opening ceremony, Performing Arts Students Ensemble (PASE) Voci tributed a chorale performance that honors the educators.
To spark joy with the event, the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) Student Council, Midwifery Student Organization (MSO), and the Nursing Student Organization (NSO) hosted games to energize and entertain the teachers.

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day]
SO), and the Nursing Student Organization (NSO) hosted games to energize and entertain the teachers.
Furthermore, the PASE Ballo sparked warmth and embrace with a performance number.
Moreover, the Supreme Student Council (SSC) and Human Resource Management Unit (HRMU) conducted an awarding ceremony to appreciate the faculty’s skills with admiration and humor.
Meanwhile, Hon. Rolen Paulino Jr., City Mayor of Olongapo, held a speech and cash raffle for the faculty.
“Salamat sa sacrifices niyo, sa mahabang pasensiya ninyo, tiyaka sa lahat ng ginagawa niyo para sa mga students natin,” Paulino stat

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-celebrates-world-teachers-day]
mahabang pasensiya ninyo, tiyaka sa lahat ng ginagawa niyo para sa mga students natin,” Paulino stated.
An Audio-Visual Presentation (AVP) video was shown to the faculty, featuring greetings and messages from the students, followed by the students who presented gifts to their beloved tutors.
Lastly, another raffle event was held with prizes for the faculty, which filled the room with joy and laughter.
The said event was supposed to be celebrated on October 3rd but was rescheduled due to Typhoon Paolo.| via Jasmine Zeah Llagas/The FOREFRONT

# DISINFECTION NOTICE
[Image: DISINFECTION NOTICE]
**Friday, October 17, 2025**
October 17, 2025
Due to the recent increase in influenza cases, the Olongapo City Health Office will conduct disinfection procedures at the Gordon College Main and Annex Buildings today, October 17, 2025, at 1:00 p.m.
Thus, afternoon work and face-to-face transactions in the buildings are suspended; classes will shift to online delivery, with all face-to-face activities scheduled to resume tomorrow, October 18, 2025.

# TF marks history at 21st LHEPC; 3 scribes snatch indiv awards
[Image: TF marks history at 21st LHEPC; 3 scribes snatch indiv awards]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, March 22, 2026**
The Forefront (TF) delegates secured three individual awards, first time in the publication’s Luzon-wide stint, at the 21st Luzon-Wide Higher Education Press Conference (LHEPC) from March 17-19, at NGN Grand Hotel in Tuguegarao City.
Representing Central Luzon, the individual category participants bagged awards in: Traditional Literary Graphics Illustration (English), 3rd place, f

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tf-marks-history-at-21st-lhepc-3-scribes-snatch-indiv-awards]
ry participants bagged awards in: Traditional Literary Graphics Illustration (English), 3rd place, from Senior Layout Artist Angelique Jose; Head of Traditional Arts Mary Isobelle Arcilla secured 7th Place in Traditional Comic Strip Drawing (English); and John Christian Rocero, former social media manager, took home 9th place in Photojournalism (English).
At the same time, John Patrick Mateo, Editor-in-Chief, participated in Devcomm Writing (Filipino) while Lance Isaac Leon, Associate Editor, participated in Editorial Writing (English) and Column Writing (English).
Moreover, Region III was nam

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tf-marks-history-at-21st-lhepc-3-scribes-snatch-indiv-awards]
rticipated in Editorial Writing (English) and Column Writing (English).
Moreover, Region III was named the Best Performing Region for the Individual and Group categories.
Announced on the same day, LHEPC will be replaced next year by the 1st National Higher Education Press Conference (NHEPC), to be hosted by Region I at Vigan City.
Similar to LHEPC rules, the top 5 placers in each category from the respective regional presscons will advance to the NHEPC. | via Roi Chester B. Manio/ THE FOREFRONT

# CEAS Mentors, ibinida ang ginto sa Standard Category
[Image: CEAS Mentors, ibinida ang ginto sa Standard Category]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, September 15, 2025**
GORDON COLLEGE, OLONGAPO CITY – Umangat ang kahusayan ng College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors sa taunang Dance Sports Competition Standard Category, matapos matagumpay na hinataw ang dalawang katunggali sa entablado sa pamamagitan ng tatlong klasikong sayaw.
Sinubukan ang kanilang husay at indak sa Waltz na nagpakita ng pino at walang-kupas na galaw, sa Tango na umigting sa m

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category]
ang husay at indak sa Waltz na nagpakita ng pino at walang-kupas na galaw, sa Tango na umigting sa matinding ekspresyon, at Quickstep na sumubok sa bilis, koordinasyon, at tibay ng bawat pares.
Sa kabila ng dikit na laban, naging kapansin-pansin ang stage presence, chemistry, kontrolado ngunit makapangyarihang galaw ni Jieann Cahanding at Ivan Russel Bahala mula sa CEAS Mentors na nagpakita ng malinis na footwork hanggang sa makabagbag na emosyon sa entablado. Sinundan ito ng CHTM (College of Hospitality and Tourism Management) Sharks sa ikalawang pwesto at CAHS (College of Allied and Health S

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category]
spitality and Tourism Management) Sharks sa ikalawang pwesto at CAHS (College of Allied and Health Studies) Tigers sa ikatlong pwesto.
“Aside from being student athletes, of course. Kami kasi ng partner ko first time partner, yung adjustment doon na tinuturuan ko siya kasi hindi rin siya talaga dance sport athlete, pero yung nakakamangha sa kaniya ay madali siya turuan, saad ni Jieann ukol sa hinarap nilang problema bago tumungtong sa entablado.
“Yung struggle namin ay ‘yong time management medyo mahirap kasi tuwing hapon lang kami nakakapag practice,” dagdag pa niya.
“Sobrang saya po na nakak

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-mentors-ibinida-ang-ginto-sa-standard-category]
o mahirap kasi tuwing hapon lang kami nakakapag practice,” dagdag pa niya.
“Sobrang saya po na nakakaiyak. As a first-timer malaking challenge sa akin yun at malaking achievement,” ayon kay Bahala patungkol sa naramdaman matapos sungkitin ang kampeyonato.
Sa pagtatapos ng patimpalak, hindi lamang talento sa pagsasayaw ang naipakita ng mga kalahok. Bawat kolehiyo ay nagbigay ng kani-kaniyang tatak—mula sa makukulay na costume, malinis na footwork, hanggang sa mga koreograpiyang may hatid na emosyon at kwentong Gcians. | via Lianne Malazarte/The FOREFRONT

**Thursday, July 10, 2025**
Gordon College’s (GC) 23rd Commencement Exercises saw a total of 1,016 passionate graduates walk in the aisle of Subic Bay Exhibition and Convention Center (SBECC) this year, donning their respective togas and sablays.
Amidst heavy rains, Cluster I filled the morning ceremony with 487 graduates from the College of Business and Accountancy (CB

label: Gordon College
content:

er I filled the morning ceremony with 487 graduates from the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) and Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM).
Meanwhile, 529 students from Cluster II’s College of Allied Health Sciences (CAHS), College of Computer Studies (CCS), and College of Education, Arts, and Sciences (CEAS) graced the stage during the afternoon rites.
The graduates are products of Free Higher Education (FHE) from the institution and taxpayers, who enjoyed it during their tenure.

# PESO-TCLASS Visits Gordon College for BSN Program Benchmarking and COPC Preparation
[Image: PESO-TCLASS Visits Gordon College for BSN Program Benchmarking and COPC Preparation]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, August 29, 2025**
Gordon College warmly welcomed the Public Employment Service Office – Tarlac Center for Learning and Skills Success (PESO-TCLASS) under the Provincial Government of Tarlac last August 26, 2025, for a benchmarking visit.
As part of their preparations to offer the Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) program and apply for a Certificate of

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/peso-tclass-visits-gordon-college-for-bsn-program-benchmarking-and-copc-preparation]
reparations to offer the Bachelor of Science in Nursing (BSN) program and apply for a Certificate of Program Compliance (COPC), the PESO-TCLASS delegation, headed by Dr. Maria Lira Reyes – PESO Manager and School Administrator, engaged with Gordon College to learn from our best practices in curriculum development, faculty qualifications, library and physical facilities, equipment, support services, and quality assurance systems. The visit provided them with valuable insights and guidance on meeting the minimum standards required for program compliance.
This knowledge-sharing initiative reflect

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/peso-tclass-visits-gordon-college-for-bsn-program-benchmarking-and-copc-preparation]
ing the minimum standards required for program compliance.
This knowledge-sharing initiative reflects Gordon College’s continued commitment to collaboration and capacity-building among academic institutions.

# Enhancing Reading Literacy Training
[Image: Enhancing Reading Literacy Training]
[Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/extension/) [RDCES](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/rdces/) [Community Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/community-extension/)
**Saturday, June 24, 2023**
A successful whole-day training was conducted through a collaborative effort involving the GC CESU, Academic Affairs, CEAS immersion instructors, and dedicated volunteers from the DepEd teaching staff. The training’s success can be attributed to the contributions of Mr. Willy R. Antigo, Ms.

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/enhancing-reading-literacy-training]
ng staff. The training’s success can be attributed to the contributions of Mr. Willy R. Antigo, Ms. Enggelyn Yandoc, Ms. Meryl Bustos, and Ms. Mara Grace Inocencio, who served as resource trainers and played crucial roles in its smooth execution. The training saw enthusiastic participation from a diverse group of student facilitators, representing ten different programs within the College of Education, Arts, and Sciences. There were 117 trainees in the morning session and 117 in the afternoon session.

# Initial Presentation of the Project METRO
[Image: Initial Presentation of the Project METRO]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Thursday, January 29, 2026**
Gordon College – College of Computer Studies (GC-CCS), in partnership with the Department of Trade and Industry (DTI) – Zambales, successfully conducted the initial product presentation of LPCC Ikot Palengke and Fair Trade Law Monitoring activities under PROJECT METRO: Monitoring Enforcement & Tracking Resource Online on January 29, 2026.
The presentation was piloted by OneDev, a team of third-year BSIT student deve

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/initial-presentation-of-the-project-metro]
 on January 29, 2026.
The presentation was piloted by OneDev, a team of third-year BSIT student developers—Marina Camaso, Paulo Cordova, Nikolai Nepomuceno, Earl Romeo Ordovez, Aaron Paul Sario, and Seann Patrick Tamondong—who showcased a prototype of a digital system designed to enhance government operations across province-wide public markets and retail establishments. The system aims to strengthen consumer protection, enforce fair trade laws, and improve efficient public service delivery through accurate data collection and monitoring.
The activity was attended by Gordon College administrat

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/initial-presentation-of-the-project-metro]
ugh accurate data collection and monitoring.
The activity was attended by Gordon College administrators, headed by Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, College President; Dr. Darwin P. Paguio, Vice President for Academic Affairs; Dr. Erlinda S. Casela-Abarintos, Vice President for Administration and Finance; Dr. Kristin Joy A. Mendoza, Vice President for Institutional Planning and External Affairs; Dr. Ediric D. Gadia, Vice President for Research Development and Community Extension Services; Dr. Precious De Castro, Coordinator of the International Affairs Unit; Prof. Ronnie D. Luy, Dean of the College of C

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/initial-presentation-of-the-project-metro]
Castro, Coordinator of the International Affairs Unit; Prof. Ronnie D. Luy, Dean of the College of Computer Studies; Prof. Armilyn T. Martinez, Assistant Dean of the College of Computer Studies; and Prof. Reynaldo G. Bautista Jr., Program Coordinator of the Bachelor of Science in Information Technology. Also present were officials from DTI Zambales, led by Mr. Neil John Fabay, Division Chief of the Consumer Protection Division, along with Ms. Marilou Benonto and Ms. Melody Tangonan, Senior Trade Industry Development Specialists.
This initiative highlights Gordon College’s commitment to innovat

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/initial-presentation-of-the-project-metro]
 Industry Development Specialists.
This initiative highlights Gordon College’s commitment to innovation and technology-driven governance, emphasizing its role in advancing the institution’s global technology and general assistance initiatives.
Gordon College extends its appreciation to all participating officials, partners, and stakeholders for their continued support in advancing development, research, and public service through technology.

[Image: Ballo and GCians Capture 1st and 2nd Runner-ups at Cultural Competition]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, December 23, 2025**
Gordon College’s (GC) Performing Arts Students Ensemble (PASE) Ballo and other students secured the 1st and 2nd runner-up titles in the Barangay Cultural Competition at Gordon Avenue, on December 20, at SM City Downtown.
Members of PASE Ballo and other GCians competed in their respective groups, representing Barangay Pag-asa and Barangay West Tapinac.
Baran

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ballo-and-gcians-capture-1st-and-2nd-runner-ups-at-cultural-competition]
 competed in their respective groups, representing Barangay Pag-asa and Barangay West Tapinac.
Barangay Pag-asa performed their Sagudsod de Mano, which led them to be placed as the first runner-up.
Meanwhile, Barangay West Tapinac earned their place as the second runner-up after grooving their way through their Inalimango performance.
Despite the wet roads due to the rain, the participants from the institution still showed their grace and effort in their renditions, embracing the Filipino culture.
The folk dance competition was a part of the Olongapo City Fiesta Celebration. | via Jasmine Zeah

**Wednesday, October 29, 2025**
WAY TO GO! Congratulations to our College on its 3-PEAT VICTORY, emerging as CHAMPION at the Philippine Statistics Quiz – Provincial Elimination! The event was held on October 28, 2025, organized by the Philippine Statistics Authority (PSA) Zambales.
We are so proud of our students who are now Qualifiers for the Regional Competition on November 6, 2025, at the Orchid Garden Resort, Pampanga:
1. Andrei Fredeluces
2. Sophia Alai

# GC produces 9 new CPAs; yields 39.13% pass rate in October CPALE
[Image: GC produces 9 new CPAs; yields 39.13% pass rate in October CPALE]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, November 4, 2025**
Nine alumni from the College of Business and Accountancy (CBA) of Gordon College (GC) were among the passers of the October Certified Public Accountant Licensure Exam (CPALE) per Professional Regulatory Board of Accountancy, on November 4.
GC garnered an overall passing rate of 39.13% from 9 passers out of 23 takers, surpassing the national passing rate of 34.02%. These c

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-produces-9-new-cpas-yields-39-13-pass-rate-in-october-cpale]
e of 39.13% from 9 passers out of 23 takers, surpassing the national passing rate of 34.02%. These constitute the 3,460 newly registered Certified Public Accountants (CPA).
Comprising this are three first-time takers and six repeater examinees.
In addition, Mike Ronnel Castro of Pampanga State University (for. Don Honorario Ventura State University) topped the examination with a 91.83% rating.
Bicol University – Daraga is the lone institution in the list of top-performing schools with 50 or more examinees with an 84.85% passing rate.

[Alumni](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/alumni/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, August 15, 2025**
GCAAI Unveils New Student Lounge at Gordon College
The Gordon College Alumni Association, Inc. (GCAAI) is proud to announce the official blessing and turnover of the new Gordon College Student Lounge. The lounge, a project lovingly referred to as the “Garden Project,” was blessed by Rev. Fr. Mike Duffin before being officially handed over to the co

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gcaai-unveils-new-student-lounge-at-gordon-college]
 “Garden Project,” was blessed by Rev. Fr. Mike Duffin before being officially handed over to the college. This new space is for students, faculty, administration, alumni, and other members of the Gordon College community.
The initiative was spearheaded by Mr. Theo Roi N. Musni, GCAAI Public Information Officer, with the full support of Mr. Joseph Angelo B. Pusing, GCAAI President, the GCAAI Board of Trustees, GCAAI working committee members, the GC Board of Trustees headed by City Mayor Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr., officials headed by College President Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, and all Gordon Col

len C. Paulino Jr., officials headed by College President Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, and all Gordon College alumni.
The blessing and turnover ceremony was attended by Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano, Gordon College President, along with the college’s Vice Presidents. This new lounge stands as a testament to the GCAAI’s commitment to giving back to its alma mater.

# GC marks second year in One Billion Rising movement to end violence against women and girls
[Image: GC marks second year in One Billion Rising movement to end violence against women and girls]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, February 16, 2026**
Gordon College marked its second consecutive year of participation in the global campaign One Billion Rising through a public awareness activity themed “Rise for our bodies, our Earth, our Future,” held at Ayala Malls Harbor Point on February 14.  
The event brought together students and mall-goers in a collective call

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-second-year-in-one-billion-rising-movement-to-end-violence-against-women-and-girls]
rbor Point on February 14.  
The event brought together students and mall-goers in a collective call to stand against violence toward women and girls while promoting empowerment and social responsibility.  
Established in 2012, One Billion Rising has developed into an international movement recognized for mobilizing communities across the world to advocate for the elimination of gender-based violence.  
The initiative is grounded in global data from the United Nations indicating that approximately one in three women experience violence at some point in their lives, representing more than a bil

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-second-year-in-one-billion-rising-movement-to-end-violence-against-women-and-girls]
ly one in three women experience violence at some point in their lives, representing more than a billion women and girls worldwide.  
The activity was led by the Institutional Planning, Development, and External Affairs (IPDEA) through the Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) Unit in partnership with the National Service Training Program (NSTP) and Ayala Malls Inc., with the goal of strengthening public awareness and encouraging community participation in the advocacy.  
One of the program’s highlights was a flash mob performance by Pase Ballo and SHAPES, who led a mass dance to the One Bill

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-second-year-in-one-billion-rising-movement-to-end-violence-against-women-and-girls]
ighlights was a flash mob performance by Pase Ballo and SHAPES, who led a mass dance to the One Billion Rising anthem, Break the Chain, symbolizing unity and resistance.  
Mr. Joseph Atencio, head of the DEI Unit, emphasized that violence against women and girls does not only occur in public spaces but is often present within homes and personal relationships.  
“Bukod sa awareness na gusto nating ipakalat, gusto nating maging mapagmatyag ‘yung mga tao. The purpose of One Billion Rising is truly to end violence against women and girls. There can be no sustainable development without women’s emp

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-second-year-in-one-billion-rising-movement-to-end-violence-against-women-and-girls]
to end violence against women and girls. There can be no sustainable development without women’s empowerment,” he remarked. | via Alyssa Marie Alcantara/The FOREFRONT

# RESCHEDULING OF ID PROCESSING (December 8, 2025)
[Image: RESCHEDULING OF ID PROCESSING (December 8, 2025)]
**Sunday, December 7, 2025**
[Image: 📢]
**NOTICE: RESCHEDULING OF ID PROCESSING**
Please be informed that the scheduled ID Processing on December 8, 2025 (Monday) has been rescheduled due to the non-working holiday.   
ID Processing will now be conducted on any day from December 9 to December 12, 2025 (Tuesday–Friday). Students may proceed on their preferred date within this period.   
We apologize for any inconvenience this may cause and appreciate your understanding.

[Image: 𝐓𝐮𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐃𝐮𝐧𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 (𝐓𝐃𝐏) 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐄𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐒𝐮𝐛𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐲 (𝐓𝐄𝐒) 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧]
**Thursday, December 11, 2025**
𝐀𝐍𝐍𝐎𝐔𝐍𝐂𝐄𝐌𝐄𝐍𝐓
𝐓𝐮𝐥𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐃𝐮𝐧𝐨𝐧𝐠 𝐏𝐫𝐨𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐦 (𝐓𝐃𝐏) 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐞𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐄𝐝𝐮𝐜𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐒𝐮𝐛𝐬𝐢𝐝𝐲 (𝐓𝐄𝐒) 𝐃𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐫𝐢𝐛𝐮𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧
To all 𝐥𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐄𝐒 𝐠𝐫𝐚𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐞𝐬, please be advised that the claiming of your financial grants will be held at 𝐆𝐨𝐫𝐝𝐨𝐧 𝐂𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐠𝐞, 𝐅𝐮𝐧𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐇𝐚𝐥𝐥 (𝟑𝐫𝐝 𝐅𝐥𝐨𝐨𝐫) 𝐨𝐧 𝐃𝐞𝐜𝐞𝐦𝐛𝐞𝐫 𝟏𝟏–𝟏𝟐, 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓, 𝐟𝐫𝐨𝐦 𝟕:𝟎𝟎 𝐀𝐌 𝐭𝐨 𝟓:𝟎𝟎 𝐏𝐌.
This payout covers the following:
• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟕.𝟏 – 𝟐𝐧𝐝 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟑–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒
• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟑 –

• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟕.𝟏 – 𝟐𝐧𝐝 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟑–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒
• 𝐓𝐃𝐏 𝐁𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡 𝟑 – 𝟏𝐬𝐭 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓
• 𝐓𝐄𝐒 𝐋𝐢𝐬𝐭𝐚𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐚𝐧 𝟏𝟓 – 𝟏𝐬𝐭 𝐒𝐞𝐦𝐞𝐬𝐭𝐞𝐫, 𝐀𝐜𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐦𝐢𝐜 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟒–𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓
[Image: 📄]

Please prepare the following complete documents :
1. 𝐓𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐩𝐡𝐨𝐭𝐨𝐜𝐨𝐩𝐢𝐞𝐬 of your 𝐬𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 𝐨𝐫 𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐝 𝐠𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐧𝐦𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 (𝐰𝐢𝐭𝐡 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞), each with 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐬𝐩𝐞𝐜𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐬𝐢𝐠𝐧𝐚𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐬
2. 𝐅𝐨𝐫 𝐀𝐮𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐳𝐞𝐝 𝐑𝐞𝐩𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐞𝐧𝐭𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐯𝐞𝐬 (𝐈𝐦𝐦𝐞𝐝𝐢𝐚𝐭𝐞 𝐅𝐚𝐦𝐢𝐥𝐲 𝐎𝐍𝐋𝐘) — all in 𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐞𝐞 (𝟑) 𝐜𝐨𝐩𝐢𝐞𝐬:
a. Original 𝐀𝐮𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐫𝐢𝐳𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐋𝐞𝐭𝐭𝐞𝐫 with the grantee’s wet signature.
b. Photocopy of the 𝐬𝐭𝐮𝐝𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐈𝐃 𝐨𝐫 𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐢𝐝 𝐠𝐨𝐯𝐞

# GC ALCUCOA Accreditation 2025
[Image: GC ALCUCOA Accreditation 2025]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, November 26, 2025**
Nagsimula na ang tatlong-araw na akreditasyon ng Association of Local Colleges and Universities Commission on Accreditation (ALCUCOA) sa Gordon College.
Ngayong taon, ang Bachelor of Science in Business Administration (BSBA) Major in Marketing Management ay nangangarap makamit ang Level III Accreditation.
Samantala, ang Bachelor of Science in Accountancy (BSA), Bachelor in Elementary Education (BEEd), Bachelor of Early Childhood Educatio

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-alcucoa-accreditation-2025]
 in Accountancy (BSA), Bachelor in Elementary Education (BEEd), Bachelor of Early Childhood Education (BECEd), Bachelor of Secondary Education Major in Filipino (BSEd-Fil), at Bachelor of Secondary Education Major in Science (BSEd-Sci) ay target naman ang Level II Accreditation.
Bukod dito, ang Bachelor of Culture and Arts Education (BCAEd) at Bachelor of Physical Education (BPEd) ay nominado bilang mga kandidato para sa akreditasyon ngayong taon.
Suportado ito ni Mayor [Atty. Rolen Paulino Jr.](https://www.facebook.com/mayorlenjpaulino?__cft__[0]=AZU3QvTqhQlkmJYMC3O-Ag6rBVcz3QYgae7wuVrFTGSN7k

Prof. Rolingel Calilung, BPED Prof. Wendell Delos Reyes, BCAED Dr. Priscilla Minas, BSA Prof. Anselmo Giron, BEED Dr. Eliz

# Ex-NSO prexy secures rank 9 spot; GC among top schools in Feb 2026 PNLE
[Image: Ex-NSO prexy secures rank 9 spot; GC among top schools in Feb 2026 PNLE]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, March 6, 2026**
Alumna Alexis Molon Penuliar of the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) ranked ninth in the February 2026 Philippine Nursing Licensure Examination (PNLE), as announced by the Professional Regulation Commission (PRC) today, March 6.
Graduated cum laude, the former Gordon College – Nursing Student Organization (GC-NSO) president earned an 89.80% rating in the

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ex-nso-prexy-secures-rank-9-spot-gc-among-top-schools-in-feb-2026-pnle]
mer Gordon College – Nursing Student Organization (GC-NSO) president earned an 89.80% rating in the recent board examination.
The institution recorded 55 passers out of 60 total takers, posting an overall passing rate of 91.67%. Comprising this are 55 out of 57 first-time passers while none of the three repeat examinees passed.
Moreover, GC was among the country’s top-performing schools at 4th rank in the recent licensure exam with 50 to 99 examinees and at least 85% passing percentage, far surpassing the national passing rate of 44.24%.
Meanwhile, Alyanna Marie Ramos Villegas of Saint Paul Un

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ex-nso-prexy-secures-rank-9-spot-gc-among-top-schools-in-feb-2026-pnle]
assing the national passing rate of 44.24%.
Meanwhile, Alyanna Marie Ramos Villegas of Saint Paul University – Tuguegarao (SPU-T) was the February 2026 PNLE’s Top 1 Passer, having 92.00% rating. SPU-T also emerged as the Top 1 Performing School with 100 or more examinees, having 99.10% passing rate with 220 successful examinees out of 222.
PRC administered the examination on February 26 and 27 in various testing centers, including Manila, Baguio, Cebu, Davao, and several key provinces such as Palawan, Laguna, and Batangas. | via Alyssa Marie Alcantara and Keane Rafael Abdon/The FOREFRONT

# Proper Usage of Glass Boards
[Image: Proper Usage of Glass Boards]
**Thursday, May 23, 2024**
GUIDELINES FOR THE USE OF GLASS BOARD INSTALLED IN THE CLASSROOMS
* Use White Board Marker only to write on it
* Always erase marker ink from the surface after each use to prevent stains and ghosting.
* Don’t use dirty eraser or cloth, it will start spread ink residue across the surface of the board.
* Glass board must be cleared of any markings before the next class starts.
* Avoid using tapes or adhesives on the glass surface. These materials may be difficult and cause permanent damage to the glas

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/proper-usage-of-glass-boards]
esives on the glass surface. These materials may be difficult and cause permanent damage to the glass board.
* Don’t use your hands to wipe a glass board! Your skin carries natural oils that over time leave a build-up of oil residue on the board. This can eventually ruin the surface of the glass board.
* Do not use sharp objects to avoid scratching or damaging the board surface.
* If the glass board is accidentally broken, it must be replaced with the same brand within seven (7) days from the date it was broken. If the item with specific brand and specification is not available, it must be rep

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/proper-usage-of-glass-boards]
te it was broken. If the item with specific brand and specification is not available, it must be replaced with three (3) timex of the quantity or in quantity comparable to the price of the item. The brand and specification must be approved first by the concerned personnel before replacement.

# LIMC History
Gordon College Library started in 1999 when the former Olongapo City Colleges, now Gordon College, put up a curricular program for Computer Studies and Business Management and Accountancy. With only a few books and mostly computer subjects in hand, Mrs. Remedios Sapuan, who was then a Utility worker, took charge of the library operation with the assistance of the Administrative Staff.
The Library moved four times to different locations in the building and its fourth was in the former KHG Hall. When the library was first opened, books were in the cabinet with glass doors and were

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
mer KHG Hall. When the library was first opened, books were in the cabinet with glass doors and were locked. The library was using the close shelf system, where stacks cannot be directly accessed by users. The students should approach the assigned staff when borrowing books and it was then the bookshelf was opened to get the book needed.
In 2002, Thelma Ramos, a clerk aid of the Assessor’s Office, was transferred to the College Library to assist Mrs. Sapuan in the library operation. According to Mrs. Sapuan, a certain instructor, in the person of Mr. Sydney Maglalang, was temporarily assigned

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
 Mrs. Sapuan, a certain instructor, in the person of Mr. Sydney Maglalang, was temporarily assigned to the library. Services of the staff in the City Library were borrowed in the processing of books acquired for the College Library. In June 2003, the first College Librarian was hired, Ms. Maria Corazon D. Maneja as a casual position. Later she was promoted to a permanent position. The problem was by her was the book organization, the right classification of books, classification tools (DDC) Dewey Decimal Classification System and there were no catalog cards and cabinets. Some of the books were

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
y Decimal Classification System and there were no catalog cards and cabinets. Some of the books were infested by termites which were already problems before but were not treated completely. Books were thenr eorganized, classified, and cataloged and were assigned new numbers using DD Classification tools that were borrowed from the Ci4ty Library. The circulation counter and grills dividing the books stacks and reading area were removed. Shelves were distributed and fastened on all library walls to make the books accessible to the users and to widen the reading area. Heavily termite-infested boo

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
o make the books accessible to the users and to widen the reading area. Heavily termite-infested books were removed and deleted from the records. New books were formed. Newspapers were compiled with the date labeled and put on display. In 2006, books from Australia were received by the library through the Rotary Club of Olongapo. Books were found to be outdated and mostly for High School and Elementary level students. In the same year, another book donation was received from Asia Foundations, Inc. Books became very useful to patrons because of their general content. In March 2007, Ms. Betty Fi

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
c. Books became very useful to patrons because of their general content. In March 2007, Ms. Betty Fielder, a friend of Mrs. Dely Aristorenas, former College Administrator, donated books and magazines for use by HRM students and faculty. Two air conditioners were installed to replace the former electric fans for ventilation and besides its small space, library patrons flocked the library especially nursing students who occupied the most number of enrollees. The library had then 8,361 volumes of books as of October 2007.
In the summer of 2010, the librarian during one of the EXECOM (ExecutiveCom

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
ooks as of October 2007.
In the summer of 2010, the librarian during one of the EXECOM (ExecutiveCommittee) meetings, suggested removing over duplicated copies of books to provide spaces for new arrivals. Enough copies from weeded books were retained. 2,958 books were discarded and returned to GSO (Government Supply Office) but the latter suggested just donating them to the City Library.
In 2012, Gordon College's 13th Founding Anniversary, a Give-a-Book Project was launched and spearheaded by the College Library to collect tertiary level books from students, faculty, alumni, and friends of

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
y the College Library to collect tertiary level books from students, faculty, alumni, and friends of Gordon College. As of this date, May 6, 2013, we have collected 182 book donations.
On August 7, 2012, when the first knee-deep flood entered the College Library, books located on the first two-row level shelves were partially damaged but were restored. But on September 23, 2013, during a chest-deep heavy flood of Olongapo City, almost 2/3 of the library materials were soaked in the mud and water and devastated and discarded. Furniture which was made of light materials, files, and newly acquire

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
 and devastated and discarded. Furniture which was made of light materials, files, and newly acquired books was included, but other computers were restored. Due to the kindness of City Mayor Rolen C. Paulino, he approved a 1 million budget for the purchase of new general education books, professional books, and journals. The first set of books was delivered in December 2013. Another era was opened for Gordon College Library for its transfer to the new building funded and built by SM in 2015-2016. The official transfer began on June 24, 2016, until July of the same year. The new and much wider

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
. The official transfer began on June 24, 2016, until July of the same year. The new and much wider library which is located on the fourth floor was opened to students on August 1, 2016, the start of the first semester, the Academic Year 2016- 2017.
Mr. Marlon A. Atencio recommended to the Executive Council the change of the library name into “Library and Instructional Media Center” for the reason that the library is now having a media center that supports the educational needs of the library and it was approved by the Executive Council spearheaded by the College President, Dr. Imelda DP. Sori

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/campus-life/library-and-instructional-media-center/limc-history]
d it was approved by the Executive Council spearheaded by the College President, Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano last May 31, 2018.

# GC Coastline Clean-up Drive
[Image: GC Coastline Clean-up Drive]
[Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/extension/) [RDCES](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/rdces/) [Community Extension](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/community-extension/)
**Friday, June 23, 2023**
On World Water Day 2023, numerous individuals joined forces with a shared goal of cleaning up the coast to protect nature and ensure clean water. This initiative served as a reminder of the importance of water conservation and environmental preservation. The GC Community Extension Services Unit (GC-CESU) and

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-coastline-clean-up-drive]
conservation and environmental preservation. The GC Community Extension Services Unit (GC-CESU) and the Office of Student Welfare and Services (OSWS) partnered with student groups to organize and carry out the cleanup. Their efforts inspired 182 student leaders, volunteers, and advisors to actively participate. Thanks to their enthusiastic involvement, the event was a resounding success.

[Image: Gordon College welcomed delegates from Norzagaray College (NC)]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, July 18, 2025**
Gordon College welcomed delegates from Norzagaray College (NC) for a benchmarking visit focused on best practices in student-developed systems, Computer Studies and Tourism and Hospitality program deliveries, and institutional innovations.
Led by Dr. Mindality Ocampo-Cruz, the NC team engaged with GC officials. The activity featured productive exchanges on system integration, aca

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-welcomed-delegates-from-norzagaray-college-nc]
eam engaged with GC officials. The activity featured productive exchanges on system integration, academic quality, research, community engagement, and accreditation.
The visit highlighted a shared commitment to educational excellence and future collaboration.

# Avoid Cellphone Use on Stairs
[Image: Avoid Cellphone Use on Stairs]
**Friday, April 17, 2026**
**Safety Reminder Regarding Cellphone Use on Stairways**  
To ensure the safety of all students and faculty, we are reminding everyone of the following guideline regarding electronic device usage on stairs:
**Policy:**  
Cellphone use is strictly prohibited while ascending or descending staircases. This includes texting, browsing social media, making calls, or playing games.
**Rationale:**  
Stairways are high-traffic areas that require full attention. Using a cellphone while walking significantly

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/avoid-cellphone-use-on-stairs]
ys are high-traffic areas that require full attention. Using a cellphone while walking significantly reduces spatial awareness and reaction time, increasing the risk of:
* Slips, trips and falls
* Collisions with other students
* Blocking the flow of traffic during passing periods
**Procedure:**
* **Pause:** If you must send a message or answer a call, step to the side and stop walking before using your device.
* **Pocket:** Keep phones stored in pockets or bags while navigating steps.
* **Proceed:** Only resume walking once the device is put away and your full attention is on the stairs.
Than

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/guideline/avoid-cellphone-use-on-stairs]
ed:** Only resume walking once the device is put away and your full attention is on the stairs.
Thank you for helping us keep our campus safe.

# CHTM Sharks sealed the set against CEAS Mentors in Men’s Singles
[Image: CHTM Sharks sealed the set against CEAS Mentors in Men’s Singles]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**
TRICKSHOT COVERED COURT—The College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks bagged the gold again during the men’s badminton singles championship match, 21-6, defeating the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors.
With his outstanding performance, Kryztofer Matias Basa of CHTM Sharks ended the finals with two straight sets, leaving no chance

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-sealed-the-set-against-ceas-mentors-in-mens-singles]
nce, Kryztofer Matias Basa of CHTM Sharks ended the finals with two straight sets, leaving no chance for his opponent to recover.
In an interview, Basa shared that he already expected the victory as he had once played with a stronger player before.
“‘Yu‘n pa yung ginamit ko na motivation para magchampion ulit yung CHTM,” he said when asked about being pressured to defend the previous win of CHTM last SIKLAB.
He also expressed his joy in playing with passion and humility. “Iniisip ko lang is maging humble, and…sportsmanship,” he added.
Moreover, the College of Computer Studies secured the bronz

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/chtm-sharks-sealed-the-set-against-ceas-mentors-in-mens-singles]
ng humble, and…sportsmanship,” he added.
Moreover, the College of Computer Studies secured the bronze medal, while the College of Business and Accountancy and the College of Allied Health got the 4th and 5th place, respectively. | via Jeliza Tadena/The FOREFRONT

# HON. ROLEN C. PAULINO JR. RECEIVES THE MODEL LOCAL CHIEF EXECUTIVE AWARD FOR 2025 FROM ALCUCOA
[Image: HON. ROLEN C. PAULINO JR. RECEIVES THE MODEL LOCAL CHIEF EXECUTIVE AWARD FOR 2025 FROM ALCUCOA]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Tuesday, December 9, 2025**
Huge congratulations to our visionary City Mayor, Hon. Rolen C. Paulino Jr., who was conferred the prestigious Model Local Chief Executive Award for 2025 by the Association of Local Colleges and Universities Commission on Accreditation (ALCUCOA) on December 9, 2025, at the Diamond Hotel in Manila.
Our City Mayor

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/hon-rolen-c-paulino-jr-receives-the-model-local-chief-executive-award-for-2025-from-alcucoa]
sion on Accreditation (ALCUCOA) on December 9, 2025, at the Diamond Hotel in Manila.
Our City Mayor received this top honor in recognition of his exemplary leadership and outstanding commitment to advancing quality and accessible higher education in Olongapo. His administration was specifically lauded for successfully elevating academic standards and modernizing campus infrastructure to secure a globally competitive educational landscape for the city’s constituents.

**Thursday, February 13, 2025**
SAN FERNANDO, Pampanga – The Forefront, the official student publication of Gordon College, garnered five awards at the 5th Assumption Outstanding Campus Press Awards (AOCPA) held at the University of the Assumption on January 27, 2025.
Thirty publications across Luzon participated in the competition, submitting a tota

# GC marks a milestone in ALCUCOA 2025
[Image: GC marks a milestone in ALCUCOA 2025]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, December 3, 2025**
The three-day program comes to an end as Gordon College (GC) conducts an exit conference for the Association of Local Colleges and Universities Commission on Accreditation (ALCUCOA) last November 28, at the Gordon College Main Building, Function Hall.
Vying for one Level III program, five Level II programs, and two candidate programs this year, the institution’s big step is a leap for development, embodying character, excell

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-a-milestone-in-alcucoa-2025]
rograms this year, the institution’s big step is a leap for development, embodying character, excellence, and service along the way.
“This is not the end,” Dr. Imelda DP. Soriano opened the exit conference of this year’s accreditation with a firm statement.
The accreditors commended the institution’s strengths including the Library Ambassadors Organization (LAO), community extension programs, and professionalism.
Meanwhile, they also recommended improvements such as stronger programs to supplement both students’ and teachers’ needs, more tangible and sustainable community extensions, and subje

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-a-milestone-in-alcucoa-2025]
nt both students’ and teachers’ needs, more tangible and sustainable community extensions, and subjects that focus on application rather than theories.
As Dr. Elizabeth Montero, the supervising accreditor said in her message, “The new oil now is consistency,” a reminder to stay true and stand into the lenses of continuous development where growth is evident and seen.
In response, Ms. Geraldine Raydanas and Mrs. Rowenalyn Sagun expressed their utmost gratitude and promised to improve in the areas mentioned.
Ending the program, Dr. Kristin Joy Mendoza, secretary of GC’s Board of Trustees, left a

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gc-marks-a-milestone-in-alcucoa-2025]
 mentioned.
Ending the program, Dr. Kristin Joy Mendoza, secretary of GC’s Board of Trustees, left a valuable statement regarding institutionalization and sustainability.
“Every accreditation is an opportunity to innovate and fill the spaces where we can do better,” Dr. Mendoza emphasized.
Meanwhile, the results of the 2025 accreditation are set to be announced in the next few weeks. | via Mikhaela Sagun/The FOREFRONT

# CCS Phoenix Clinches Gold at SIKLAB 2025 E-Games
[Image: CCS Phoenix Clinches Gold at SIKLAB 2025 E-Games]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, September 17, 2025**
FUNCTION HALL, GORDON COLLEGE MAIN BLDG.—The Gordon College Main Building Function Hall buzzed with excitement as SIKLAB 2025 E-Games came to a spectacular close, featuring intense battles for gold, silver, and bronze.
In the Battle for Bronze, the College of Education, Arts, and Science (CEAS) Mentors defeated the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) Tigers, 22-8, securing third place and bringi

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ccs-phoenix-clinches-gold-at-siklab-2025-e-games]
s defeated the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) Tigers, 22-8, securing third place and bringing pride to the CEAS community.
Meanwhile, the championship series between the CCS Phoenix and the CHTM Sharks was a nail-biter in a best-of-three format. CCS edged CHTM in Game 1, 21-20, followed by a 17-win in Game 2. In the final showdown, the Phoenix dominated, 12-1, clinching the gold medal with an overall 2-1 victory.
The CHTM Sharks earned the silver medal, demonstrating remarkable perseverance and strategy throughout the finals.
SIKLAB 2025 E-Games concluded as a remarkable showcase of d

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ccs-phoenix-clinches-gold-at-siklab-2025-e-games]
ance and strategy throughout the finals.
SIKLAB 2025 E-Games concluded as a remarkable showcase of determination, strategy, and camaraderie, crowning the CCS Phoenix as champions while bringing the excitement of competitive E-sports to Gordon College—and leaving fans eagerly anticipating next year’s edition. | via Frances Marie Farne/The FOREFRONT

# TF SECURES LHEPC SPOT
[Image: TF SECURES LHEPC SPOT]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, February 14, 2026**
The Forefront journos once again demonstrated their grit and skills in campus journalism, securing several awards at the 24th Regional Higher Education Press Conference (RHEPC), held at Palm Cabanas Resort, Arayat, Pampanga, from February 11 to 13.
Two of the publication’s outgoing executive members, John Patrick Mateo (Editor-in-Chief) and Lance Isaac Leon (Associate Editor), were recognized as Outstanding Tertiary Campus Journalists (OTCJ) in Region II

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tf-secures-lhepc-spot]
n (Associate Editor), were recognized as Outstanding Tertiary Campus Journalists (OTCJ) in Region III.
In the group category, the pub’s entry for Opening Billboard ranked 4th Place overall.
In individual categories, delegates from the publication bagged nine (9) awards as listed below.
Angelique Jose
1st Place – Traditional Literary Graphics Illustration (English)
Lance Isaac Leon
2nd Place – Editorial Writing (English)
3rd Place – Opinion Writing (English)
John Patrick Mateo
3rd Place – Devcom Writing (Filipino)
John Christian Rocero
3rd Place – Photojournalism (English)
Mary Isobelle Arcilla

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tf-secures-lhepc-spot]
Writing (Filipino)
John Christian Rocero
3rd Place – Photojournalism (English)
Mary Isobelle Arcilla
5th Place – Traditional Comic Strip Drawing (English)
6th Place – Traditional Editorial Cartooning (English)
Keane Rafael Abdon
8th Place – News Writing (English)
Mikhaela Sagun
10th Place – Poetry Writing (English)
Overall, The Forefront is the 11th Highest Pointer in individual categories against 42 participating college publications in Central Luzon.
Additionally, Keane Rafael Abdon is the newly elected division coordinator of Olongapo City for the Young Journalist Association of Region III

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tf-secures-lhepc-spot]
ly elected division coordinator of Olongapo City for the Young Journalist Association of Region III (YJAR-III).
Five campus journalists from the publication, who garnered top 5 placements in their respective categories, will advance to the 21st Luzon-Wide Higher Education Press Conference (LHEPC) in Tuguegarao City, Cagayan, next month to compete in eight categories. | via Mikhaela Sagun and Lance Isaac Leon, THE FOREFRONT

# Gordon College Welcomes IYES Language School for Cultural Exchange and Nihongo Lesson
[Image: Gordon College Welcomes IYES Language School for Cultural Exchange and Nihongo Lesson]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Monday, March 9, 2026**
Last March 6, 2026, Gordon College, through its International Affairs and Linkages Office, welcomed visitors from IYES Language School. Two Japanese students conducted an engaging educational and cultural exchange with 2nd Year BS Tourism Management students taking THP101 – Foreign Language 1 under Ms. Jacqueline Fabian, Instructor. T

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-welcomes-iyes-language-school-for-cultural-exchange-and-nihongo-lesson]
sm Management students taking THP101 – Foreign Language 1 under Ms. Jacqueline Fabian, Instructor. The class visit featured a mini cultural presentation and an introductory Japanese (Nihongo) lesson, where the visiting students shared insights into Japanese culture, including common conversations and greetings, as well as interesting Japanese onomatopoeic expressions used in daily life. The highlight of the session was the interactive Q&A activity, where students enthusiastically exchanged questions and ideas, creating a lively and meaningful dialogue between cultures. This initiative aimed to

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-welcomes-iyes-language-school-for-cultural-exchange-and-nihongo-lesson]
ions and ideas, creating a lively and meaningful dialogue between cultures. This initiative aimed to foster cross-cultural understanding, provide opportunities for both visitors and students to share experiences and perspectives, and create a warm, interactive, and memorable learning experience for everyone involved.

# Shift to Online Classes (July 19, 2025)
[Image: Shift to Online Classes (July 19, 2025)]
**Friday, July 18, 2025**
**ANNOUNCEMENT**
July 18, 2025
SHIFT TO ONLINE CLASSES ADVISORY
Due to inclement weather, please be informed that all classes tomorrow, July 19, 2025, will shift to online modality, including Related Learning Experiences (RLE).
Face-to-face classes will resume on Monday, July 21, 2025.

# TF’s Luto, among top newsletters at 2026 AOCPA; scribes bag 4 individual awards
[Image: TF’s Luto, among top newsletters at 2026 AOCPA; scribes bag 4 individual awards]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, March 7, 2026**
he Forefront, the official student publication of Gordon College, took home the 8th Most Outstanding Campus Newsletter and four individual awards at the 6th Assumption Outstanding Campus Press Awards (AOCPA).
The event, hosted by the University of the Assumption’s Regina, took place in San Fernando, Pampanga, on February 27.
The first semestral

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tfs-luto-among-top-newsletters-at-2026-aocpa-scribes-bag-4-individual-awards]
f the Assumption’s Regina, took place in San Fernando, Pampanga, on February 27.
The first semestral issue of The Forefront, Luto, was one of this year’s recipients of Most Outstanding Campus Newsletter.
During the awards ceremony, the publication’s Editor-in-Chief John Patrick Mateo earned the 2nd Most Outstanding Campus Journalist award.
Opinion-Editorial Editor Stephanie Estrella’s piece, “Pundido ang ilaw sa susunod na kalsada”, made her the 4th Most Outstanding Editorial Writer and Punto Central Luzon’s Choice for Most Outstanding Editorial Writer.
Meanwhile, Head of Visual Arts Maryisobe

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tfs-luto-among-top-newsletters-at-2026-aocpa-scribes-bag-4-individual-awards]
ntral Luzon’s Choice for Most Outstanding Editorial Writer.
Meanwhile, Head of Visual Arts Maryisobelle Arcilla was named the 10th Most Outstanding Editorial Cartoonist in another Luzon-wide stint.
The annual awards, featuring 46 publications from various regions in the north, saw The Forefront emerge as one of Luzon’s best.

# CEAS Women’s Historic Smash Hit: Another Gold in Futsal Match
[Image: CEAS Women’s Historic Smash Hit: Another Gold in Futsal Match]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, September 19, 2025**
What a thrilling clash between pink and blue.
The College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) Mentors defeated College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) Sharks after a long showdown that ended in 3-0, and finished as champions in the Women’s Futsal match on the last day of SIKLAB 2025, held at New Asinan covered court.
The Mentors did not let their guard down eve

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/ceas-womens-historic-smash-hit-another-gold-in-futsal-match]
t day of SIKLAB 2025, held at New Asinan covered court.
The Mentors did not let their guard down ever since their first game, making sure everything is coordinated to one another until the final match.
Cheers roared through the court as the intensifying moments of the game went on, with Mentors maintaining their momentum throughout the match.
Despite the Sharks’ efforts to lessen the pressure, CEAS women clocked the game with a winning score of 3-0. | via Mariah Llave/The FOREFRONT

[Image: GC-SSC benchmarks student leadership and governance among HEIs]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, November 19, 2025**
In line with the commitment of the Student Council in upholding the inherent rights and cardinal necessities of the academic community, the 𝗚𝗼𝗿𝗱𝗼𝗻 𝗖𝗼𝗹𝗹𝗲𝗴𝗲 – 𝗦𝘂𝗽𝗿𝗲𝗺𝗲 𝗦𝘁𝘂𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘁 𝗖𝗼𝘂𝗻𝗰𝗶𝗹 (GC-SSC) conducts a benchmarking activity on best practices, strategies for student leadership development, and organizational dynamics on November 17-18, 2025, at 𝘾𝙚𝙣𝙩𝙧𝙖𝙡 𝙇𝙪𝙯𝙤𝙣 𝙎𝙩𝙖𝙩𝙚 𝙐𝙣𝙞𝙫𝙚𝙧𝙨𝙞𝙩𝙮

[Image: Gordon College Named Top 4 Performing School in Feb 2026 NLE with 91.67% Passing Rate]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Saturday, March 7, 2026**
Huge congratulations to the College of Allied Health Studies (CAHS) for its outstanding performance in the recently conducted Nursing Licensure Examination (NLE) held on February 26–27, 2026. Gordon College has officially secured its spot as the TOP 4 PERFORMING SCHOOL with a remarkable institutional passing rate of 91.67%. This ach

label: Gordon College

spot as the TOP 4 PERFORMING SCHOOL with a remarkable institutional passing rate of 91.67%. This achievement is particularly exceptional as it more than doubles the National Passing Rate of 44.24%, proving once again that our educational standards are truly world-class. Alongside this institutional success, we celebrate the individual brilliance of Ms. Alexis Molon Penuliar, RN, for clinching the TOP 9 spot with a stellar rating of 89.80%.
To all our newly Registered Nurses, we are incredibly proud of your hard work and dedication!

[NSTP](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/nstp/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, June 8, 2025**
Two thousand Gordon College (GC) students enrolled in their National Service and Training Program (NSTP) courses filled the SMX Convention Center, Olongapo City, on Wednesday, for this year’s NSTP Graduation Ceremony.
With the theme, “Volunteerism in Action: Shaping Leaders, Strengthening Community”, this year’s batch of NSTP graduates was graced by Miss Manila 2014 Dr.

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/2000-change-makers-attend-nstp-grad-25]
s, Strengthening Community”, this year’s batch of NSTP graduates was graced by Miss Manila 2014 Dr. Krischelle D. Halili.
Dr. Halili emphasized that leadership is an “act not for yourself, but for others” in her address. “Serve something greater than yourself and take your friends with you.”
Moreover, she called on students to love the country and God while also channeling ‘tech natives’ to participate in online community initiatives as well as community services and university council activities on-site.
Leading the initial confirmation of the graduates, Vice President for Academic Affairs Dr

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/2000-change-makers-attend-nstp-grad-25]
s on-site.
Leading the initial confirmation of the graduates, Vice President for Academic Affairs Dr. Darwin P. Paguio also delivered his special message, focusing on the impact of genuine leadership.
“True leadership is not defined merely by title or accolades but by the impact you make in the lives of others,” he said as he congratulated the “two thousand change-makers” afterward.
This was followed by the awarding of certificates to Literary Training Service (LTS) and Civic Welfare Training Service (CWTS) completers from the different departments of the institution.
NSTP Instructor Mr. Huber

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/2000-change-makers-attend-nstp-grad-25]
rvice (CWTS) completers from the different departments of the institution.
NSTP Instructor Mr. Hubert Lew Magrata delivered his closing remarks to officially close the program.

# Bagong Lakan at Lakambini ng Kasaysayan, pinasinayaan sa Gordon College
[Image: Bagong Lakan at Lakambini ng Kasaysayan, pinasinayaan sa Gordon College]
[CEAS](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/tag/ceas/) [News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Sunday, September 7, 2025**
GORDON COLLEGE—Inukit na ang mga bagong lakan at lakambini ng kasaysayan kasabay ng panapos na selebrasyon ng Buwan ng Kasaysayan 2025 na may temang “Diwa ng Kasaysayan, Kabilin sa Kabataan” na idinaos noong Agosto 30, sa Gordon College Physical Education (PE) Hall sa pangunguna ng Samahan ng mga Mag-aaral sa

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college]
osto 30, sa Gordon College Physical Education (PE) Hall sa pangunguna ng Samahan ng mga Mag-aaral sa Araling Panlipunan (SMAP).
Nagwagi sina G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales mula sa BSEd Soc 3-A at Bb. Angel Mae Deseo mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A para sa Lakan at Lakambini ng Kasaysayan 2025.
Nasungkit naman nina G. Jhan Girbaud Hipolito mula sa BSEd Soc 2-A at Bb. Jhade Aizelle Quiocho mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A ang korona bilang Ginoong Unang Gantimpala at Binibining Unang Gantimpala.
Namayagpag din bilang Ginoong Ikalawang Gantimpala at Binibining Ikalawang Gantimpala sina G. Rafael Ellado mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A a

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college]
Ikalawang Gantimpala at Binibining Ikalawang Gantimpala sina G. Rafael Ellado mula sa BSEd Soc 1-A at Bb. Gervy Sarmiento mula sa BSEd Soc 2-A.
Bukod sa Lakan at Lakambini 2025, iginawad din ang mga espesyal na parangal tulad ng Lunting Diwa at Best in Talent na parehong nakuha nina G. Jhan Girbaud Hipolito at Bb. Jhade Aizelle Quiocho.
Nakamit naman nina G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales at Bb. Jhade Aizelle Quiocho ang mga titulong Best in Formal Attire at People’s Choice Award.
Samantala, inuwi naman nina Bb. Angel Mae Deseo at G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales ang karangalang Binibini at Ginoo ng Nakaraan.

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college]
nina Bb. Angel Mae Deseo at G. Lorenzen Rob Gonzales ang karangalang Binibini at Ginoo ng Nakaraan.
“Nung sinabi na ako yung panalo, naging proud ako kasi nagbunga yung suporta ng pamilya at mga kaibigan, higit sa lahat, nagbunga yung effort at tiwala ko sa sarili” saad ng Lakan 2025.
“In-expect ko talaga na may chance akong manalo.” Ibinahagi din ni Lorenzen na pinaghirapan niya ang kanyang preparasyon, mula ensayo hanggang pagpapakilala at pagsagot sa tanong ng mga hurado.
Samantala, labis naman ang pasasalamat ni Bb. Angel Mae Deseo, Lakambini 2025, sa lahat ng tumulong sa kanya lalo na at

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/bagong-lakan-at-lakambini-ng-kasaysayan-pinasinayaan-sa-gordon-college]
an ang pasasalamat ni Bb. Angel Mae Deseo, Lakambini 2025, sa lahat ng tumulong sa kanya lalo na at noong panahong iyon ay masama pa ang kanyang karamdaman.
“Tinrangkaso po ako hanggang sa mismong laban, sa kabila nun ay ipinagpatuloy ko ang laban sapagkat nakikita ko ang aking mga kamag-aral (SOC 1-A) na nagtutulong-tulong upang matapos ang lahat ng aming kakailanganin,” saad ni Angel Mae Deseo.
“Lahat ng pagod at puyat ay naging makabuluhan sapagkat nasungkit namin ang korona at karangalan,” aniya. | via Lovely Doroin/The FOREFRONT

# Gordon College Joins the 2025 Times Higher Education Impact Rankings
[Image: Gordon College Joins the 2025 Times Higher Education Impact Rankings]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Wednesday, June 18, 2025**
We are proud to announce that Gordon College has officially earned a place in the prestigious Times Higher Education (THE) Impact Rankings 2025! Despite being a newcomer to the global stage, GC was ranked among the top 1,500+ out of 2,526 higher education institutions (HEIs) from 130 countries, assessed for their contributions to achieving the United Nations Sustai

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-joins-the-2025-times-higher-education-impact-rankings]
s (HEIs) from 130 countries, assessed for their contributions to achieving the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UN SDGs)—a recognition based on universities’ social impact through research, stewardship, outreach, and teaching.
Gordon College participated in four SDG categories and earned the following standings:
• SDG 3 – Good Health and Well-being: Ranked 1001–1500
• SDG 4 – Quality Education: Ranked 1501+
• SDG 10 – Reduced Inequalities: Ranked 1001+
• SDG 17 – Partnerships for the Goals: Ranked 1501+
More than just recognition, this milestone reflects our evolving vision—to bec

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-joins-the-2025-times-higher-education-impact-rankings]
e Goals: Ranked 1501+
More than just recognition, this milestone reflects our evolving vision—to become a globally recognized local institution making meaningful contributions to global progress through inclusive, equitable, and sustainable development in higher education.

# Gordon College Faculty Join Basic FSL Training to Promote Inclusive Education
[Image: Gordon College Faculty Join Basic FSL Training to Promote Inclusive Education]
[News](https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/category/news/)
**Friday, August 29, 2025**
Faculty members from the College of Education, Arts and Sciences (CEAS) and the College of Hospitality and Tourism Management (CHTM) with Mr. Joseph Atencio-OIC, Head of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) Unit proudly took part in a three-day Basic Filipino Sign Language (FSL) Training held last August 26–28, 2025 at the SMX Convention Center,

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-faculty-join-basic-fsl-training-to-promote-inclusive-education]
ic Filipino Sign Language (FSL) Training held last August 26–28, 2025 at the SMX Convention Center, Olongapo City. The event was organized by the Olongapo City Persons with Disability Affairs Office (PDAO) in collaboration with the Department of Education – Olongapo City Division Office.
Throughout the training, participants engaged in interactive and hands-on activities designed to equip educators with the skills to communicate effectively with the Deaf—nurturing a more inclusive, and compassionate learning environment.
This initiative not only strengthened our instructors’ professional compe

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/gordon-college-faculty-join-basic-fsl-training-to-promote-inclusive-education]
nate learning environment.
This initiative not only strengthened our instructors’ professional competencies but also reaffirmed their dedication to accessibility, diversity, and inclusivity in education—a true reflection of Gordon College’s commitment to ensuring that no learner is left behind.

# ONLINE Adding/Dropping of Classes
[Image: ONLINE Adding/Dropping of Classes]
**Monday, January 19, 2026**
**ANNOUNCEMENT:**
**ONLINE Adding/Dropping of Classes**
*Second Semester Academic Year 2025-2026*
**January 19-30, 2026**
[Image: ☑️]
Step 1
Log-on to the student portal ([https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/gces/student](https://gordoncollegeccs.edu.ph/gces/student?fbclid=IwZXh0bgNhZW0CMTAAYnJpZBExZWpzSlY3MHlQdVJWaXA0bnNydGMGYXBwX2lkEDIyMjAzOTE3ODgyMDA4OTIAAR5DCtJJZeE0IeMkAlOWYkAjlJ5HTa_uyuxL5M64lTq3dzJieHkPXJaQ0YalBg_aem_XfOZPmWrrx4p7jcYpDLqDg))
Click Add/Drop Request Button under Menu Ite

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/online-adding-dropping-of-classes-3]
5M64lTq3dzJieHkPXJaQ0YalBg_aem_XfOZPmWrrx4p7jcYpDLqDg))
Click Add/Drop Request Button under Menu Items.
[Image: ☑️]
Step 2
To request for Dropping of classes, under Current Schedule section, click Drop button.
To request for adding of classes, under Add Subjects section, type the detail of the class (you can search by class code, subject or by description) on the search box. Click Add button of the desired class.
[Image: ☑️]
Step 3
Your selected subject(s) will appear under List of Subjects to Add/Drop section. Repeat step 2 to add or drop more classes.
Click submit to finalize your request or

label: Gordon College
content:
[Source: https://gordoncollege.edu.ph/w3/announcement/online-adding-dropping-of-classes-3]
dd/Drop section. Repeat step 2 to add or drop more classes.
Click submit to finalize your request or cancel to clear selected classes.
[Image: ☑️]
Step 4
Your request will be evaluated by your program coordinator and will be verified by the college registrar.
Regularly visit the GCES portal for request updates.
